tickets.title: Project skeleton and dependency baseline
tickets.summary: Create the initial Python project skeleton, dependency set, and directory layout for hub, node-agent, and shared runtime code.
acceptance: Python project metadata and dependency plan are defined
Initial `src/hub`, `src/node_agent`, and `src/shared` structure is laid out
The ticket leaves the repo ready for domain-specific foundation work

artifacts.summary: Implementation plan for SETUP-001: Project skeleton and dependency baseline. Defines pyproject.toml structure, src/ directory layout for hub/node_agent/shared packages, pytest and ruff configuration, and validation steps.
Implementation summary for SETUP-001: Created project skeleton with pyproject.toml, src/hub, src/node_agent, src/shared structure, pytest/ruff configs, and .gitignore.

Code review for SETUP-001: Approved. All 25 planned files created, pyproject.toml properly configured, Python 3.11+ requirement met, code quality verified with modern type hints and proper docstrings.
QA verification for SETUP-001: All acceptance criteria met, validation commands passed, lint issues fixed.

tickets.title: Shared schemas, config loading, and structured logging
tickets.summary: Establish shared Pydantic models, configuration loading patterns, trace-id propagation, and structured logging helpers used by both the hub and node agents.
acceptance: Shared request/response models are planned for hub and node-agent boundaries
Configuration loading pattern is defined for runtime services
Structured logging conventions match the canonical brief

artifacts.summary: Implementation plan for SETUP-002: Shared schemas, config loading, and structured logging. Defines Pydantic models for hub↔node communication, configuration patterns with pydantic-settings validation, full structured logging with trace-ID propagation and secret redaction, and FastAPI exception handling middleware.

Implementation plan for SETUP-002: Shared schemas, config loading, and structured logging. Defines Pydantic models for hub↔node communication, configuration patterns with pydantic-settings validation, full structured logging with trace-ID propagation and secret redaction, and FastAPI exception handling middleware.

Implementation of SETUP-002: Created shared schemas, config loading, and structured logging. New files: schemas.py (ToolRequest/Response models), context.py (trace-ID propagation), middleware.py (FastAPI handlers). Modified: models.py (field validation), config.py (validation), logging.py (JSON output, redaction), exceptions.py (trace_id support), hub/config.py, node_agent/config.py. All validation tests pass.

Code review for SETUP-002: Approved. All planned files created, type hints complete, docstrings present, trace-ID propagation works via contextvars, redaction logic handles nested structures, config validation comprehensive, middleware integration correct. Minor observation on trace context mutation pattern.

QA verification for SETUP-002: All acceptance criteria met - shared schemas, config loading, and structured logging all implemented correctly. Code inspection passed. Blocker noted: bash permission prevents runtime validation.

tickets.title: Async SQLite persistence and migration baseline
tickets.summary: Define the async SQLite layer, migration strategy, and shared persistence helpers for registries, history, and issue tracking.
acceptance: SQLite access pattern uses `aiosqlite`
Initial runtime tables are identified
Migration approach is explicit and reusable

artifacts.summary: Implementation plan for SETUP-003: Async SQLite persistence and migration baseline. Defines async connection management with aiosqlite, complete table schemas for nodes/repos/write_targets/llm_services/tasks/issues, reusable migration system with version tracking, and CRUD repository classes for each table.

Implementation of SETUP-003: Created async SQLite persistence layer with aiosqlite, 7 runtime tables (nodes, repos, write_targets, llm_services, tasks, issues, schema_version), migration system with version tracking, and CRUD repository classes for each table. All validation tests passed.

Code review for SETUP-003: Approved. Implementation matches plan, code quality is high with complete type hints and docstrings, table schemas correct with proper foreign keys and indexes, migration system is explicit and reusable, all 6 repository classes implemented with full CRUD. Minor observations noted: transaction() pattern and missing async context manager protocol - both are enhancement opportunities, not blockers.
QA verification for SETUP-003: All 3 acceptance criteria verified - aiosqlite usage, 7 runtime tables defined, migration system is explicit and reusable.

tickets.title: FastAPI hub app shell and MCP transport baseline
tickets.summary: Create the initial FastAPI application shell for the hub, including startup structure, dependency injection, and MCP-facing request routing primitives.
acceptance: Hub app entrypoint and package shape are defined
MCP-facing transport boundary is explicit
Startup path leaves room for policy and registry injection

artifacts.summary: Implementation plan for SETUP-004: FastAPI hub app shell and MCP transport baseline. Defines complete FastAPI app with lifespan, dependency injection for database/config/logging, MCP transport layer with tool handler primitives, tool routing registry, and MCP-facing HTTP endpoints.

Implementation of SETUP-004: FastAPI hub app shell and MCP transport baseline. Created dependencies.py, lifespan.py, transport layer (base.py, mcp.py, __init__.py), tool_router.py, and completed main.py, mcp.py, routes.py, config.py. All lint checks pass.

Code review for SETUP-004: Approved. Implementation matches plan - all 6 new files created, all 4 files modified as planned. Acceptance criteria satisfied: hub app entrypoint defined, MCP transport boundary explicit, startup path leaves room for policy/registry injection. Code quality high with complete type hints and docstrings.
QA verification for SETUP-004: All 3 acceptance criteria verified - hub app entrypoint, MCP transport boundary, and startup injection hooks all pass. Code quality confirmed via static analysis.

tickets.title: Test, lint, and local validation scaffold
tickets.summary: Set up the repository validation baseline so future tickets can run pytest and ruff with predictable local conventions.
acceptance: Validation commands are documented and wired
Test package layout is planned
Ruff and pytest expectations match the brief

artifacts.summary: Implementation plan for SETUP-005: Test, lint, and local validation scaffold. Defines validation commands (Makefile, CLI entrypoints), test package layout (hub/node_agent/shared directories), pytest fixtures (async_db_session, test_client, mock registries), and documentation (docs/validation.md). All acceptance criteria addressed - no blockers.

Implementation of SETUP-005: Created validation scaffold with Makefile, validation scripts (validate.py, run_tests.py, run_lint.py), test package organization (hub/node_agent/shared directories), extended conftest.py with fixtures, and docs/validation.md documentation.

Code review for SETUP-005: Approved. Implementation matches the plan exactly - all validation commands wired, test package structure created with proper fixtures, Makefile targets functional, and documentation complete. All three acceptance criteria satisfied.

QA verification for SETUP-005: Test, lint, and local validation scaffold. All 3 acceptance criteria passed - validation commands wired, test package layout correct, ruff and pytest configured properly.

tickets.title: Node registry and node health model
tickets.summary: Implement the hub-side node registry schema, CRUD path, and health metadata used to track managed machines.
acceptance: Node registry schema is defined
Health metadata model is explicit
Unknown nodes fail closed

tickets.title: Repo, write-target, and LLM service registries
tickets.summary: Define the structured registries for repos, markdown write targets, and LLM service aliases so later tools can validate every target explicitly.
acceptance: Repo registry model exists
Write-target registry model exists
LLM service alias model exists

tickets.title: Node agent service skeleton
tickets.summary: Create the node-agent service shell with config loading, health endpoint shape, and bounded executor structure for local operations.
acceptance: Node-agent package shape is defined
Health endpoint contract is explicit
Executor boundary is separate from hub code

tickets.title: Hub-to-node client, auth, and health polling
tickets.summary: Implement the authenticated hub-to-node HTTP client, timeout policy, and polling path used to reach node agents over Tailscale.
acceptance: Hub-to-node auth model is enforced
HTTP client timeouts are explicit
Health polling integrates with the node registry

tickets.title: Policy engine and normalized path validation
tickets.summary: Build the fail-closed policy engine for nodes, repos, write targets, service aliases, and normalized file paths.
acceptance: Unknown targets are rejected explicitly
Path normalization rules are central and reusable
Write and read scopes are separated cleanly

tickets.title: MCP tool routing framework
tickets.summary: Create the hub-side tool routing framework that maps exposed MCP tools to validated execution paths and shared response formatting.
acceptance: Tool registration boundary is defined
Routing integrates policy checks before execution
Shared error formatting follows the MCP-safe contract

tickets.title: list_nodes and list_repos tools
tickets.summary: Implement the top-level discovery tools that expose registered nodes and their approved repos to ChatGPT.
acceptance: Nodes list with health metadata is available
Repo discovery reflects approved registry state only
Unauthorized targets are excluded

tickets.title: inspect_repo_tree and read_repo_file tools
tickets.summary: Implement bounded repo tree inspection and file reads through the node-agent path with strict repo and path validation.
acceptance: Repo tree inspection is scoped to approved repos
File reads reject traversal and unknown paths
Responses are structured for MCP use

tickets.title: search_repo and git_status tools
tickets.summary: Implement text search and git status access through the node agent using bounded subprocess execution and structured result formatting.
acceptance: Search uses bounded ripgrep execution
Git status is exposed read-only
Timeout and error handling are explicit

tickets.title: write_markdown with atomic scoped writes
tickets.summary: Implement the markdown delivery path with approved write targets, extension allowlists, content hashing, and atomic writes.
acceptance: Writes are restricted to approved targets
Atomic write behavior is explicit
Write responses include verification metadata

tickets.title: chat_llm base routing and service registry integration
tickets.summary: Implement the core `chat_llm` routing path so the hub can validate aliases and dispatch prompts to approved backends.
acceptance: Service aliases are validated before use
Hub-to-service routing path is explicit
Structured latency and outcome metadata are planned

tickets.title: OpenCode adapter and session-aware coding-agent routing
tickets.summary: Add the OpenCode-specific adapter and session support for coding-agent workflows.
acceptance: OpenCode backend routing is explicit
Session handling is planned
Coding-agent responses fit the shared tool contract

tickets.title: Helper-model and embedding-service adapters
tickets.summary: Add adapter support for helper-class models and the dedicated embedding service used by the context pipeline.
acceptance: Helper-model alias routing is defined
Embedding-service adapter contract is explicit
Timeout and failure behavior are planned

tickets.title: Qdrant integration and context storage schema
tickets.summary: Create the shared Qdrant integration, collection layout, and structured metadata schema for project context storage.
acceptance: Qdrant client pattern is defined
Collection naming and payload schema are explicit
Structured metadata for indexed files and issues is planned

tickets.title: index_repo pipeline and content-hash tracking
tickets.summary: Implement the indexing pipeline that reads repo content, generates embeddings, and keeps indexing idempotent with content hashes.
acceptance: Indexing pipeline flow is explicit
Content-hash based reindex rules are defined
Deleted and changed files are handled deliberately

tickets.title: get_project_context and known-issue records
tickets.summary: Implement semantic project context retrieval and structured known-issue management over the indexed repo state.
acceptance: Context retrieval returns provenance metadata
Known-issue records have a structured schema
Repo access checks still apply to retrieved context

tickets.title: Context bundles and recurring-issue workflows
tickets.summary: Add higher-order context assembly such as task-oriented bundles and recurring-issue aggregation over existing context data.
acceptance: Context bundle output shape is planned
Recurring-issue aggregation rules are defined
Bundle provenance is preserved

tickets.title: Cross-repo search and global context query
tickets.summary: Implement global context and search flows that aggregate approved results across multiple indexed repos.
acceptance: Cross-repo query path is defined
Per-repo access controls still apply
Returned results keep repo-level provenance

tickets.title: Repo relationships and landscape metadata
tickets.summary: Track repo-to-repo relationships and shared metadata needed for landscape views and architecture-level navigation.
acceptance: Relationship metadata model is explicit
Landscape metadata has a structured owner
Cross-repo views can cite their source records

tickets.title: Architecture map and project landscape outputs
tickets.summary: Expose higher-level architecture and landscape outputs that combine cross-repo context with relationship metadata.
acceptance: Architecture map output shape is defined
Landscape views cite source repos and metadata
Output stays bounded to approved repos

tickets.title: Task classification and routing policy
tickets.summary: Define how GPTTalker classifies task types and selects between approved LLM and execution backends.
acceptance: Task classes are explicit
Routing policy uses registered backend metadata
Fallback expectations are defined

tickets.title: Distributed scheduler, node selection, and fallback
tickets.summary: Implement the distributed scheduler that considers task type, node health, service availability, and fallback rules.
acceptance: Scheduler inputs are explicit
Node and backend health affect routing decisions
Fallback behavior is defined and bounded

tickets.title: Task history, generated-doc log, and audit schema
tickets.summary: Implement the structured task-history, generated-doc logging, and audit schema needed for full observability.
acceptance: Task history schema is defined
Generated-doc history has an owner
Audit records include trace metadata

tickets.title: Task detail, doc history, and issue timeline tools
tickets.summary: Expose observability-facing tools for task details, generated document history, and issue timelines.
acceptance: Observability tool outputs are structured
Task-detail lookups cite persisted records
Issue timeline reads from the canonical history store

tickets.title: Cloudflare Tunnel integration and public-edge config
tickets.summary: Define and implement the public-edge path through Cloudflare Tunnel so ChatGPT can reach the hub over HTTPS without exposing inbound ports.
acceptance: Tunnel configuration owner is explicit
HTTPS public-edge boundary is documented in code and config
Security constraints remain aligned with the brief

tickets.title: Node registration bootstrap and operator config docs
tickets.summary: Add the operator-facing bootstrap path for bringing nodes online, registering them, and documenting the edge and connectivity setup.
acceptance: Node bootstrap flow is explicit
Operator config requirements are documented
Registration flow aligns with the node registry and public-edge setup

tickets.title: Contract tests for MCP tools and failure modes
tickets.summary: Build the higher-level contract tests that prove the exposed GPTTalker tools behave correctly under expected and failure-path scenarios.
acceptance: Tool contracts are covered by tests
Failure-mode tests are explicit
Validation remains aligned with the canonical brief

tickets.title: Security regression tests and redaction hardening
tickets.summary: Harden the security and observability surfaces with targeted regression tests for path traversal, secret redaction, and fail-closed behavior.
acceptance: Security regression suite covers path and target validation
Log redaction behavior is tested
Fail-closed expectations are enforced under error conditions

tickets.title: README, API docs, and handoff hardening
tickets.summary: Finalize repository-facing documentation, API references, and handoff surfaces so the finished project is easy to operate and resume.
acceptance: README reflects the implemented system
API and tool docs are aligned with reality
Handoff surfaces remain concise and current
